---
title: "Learning Diary"
subtitle: "Remotely Sensing Cities and Environments"
author: "Sheng Li"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
---

# Welcome

This is my learning diary for **CASA0023: Remotely Sensing Cities and Environments**.

The diary is organised by week. Each entry follows the same structure:

-   **Summary** – key ideas from the lecture/practical, in my own words
-   **Applications** – examples of how these ideas are used (papers, policy, real-world cases)
-   **Reflection** – what I understood well, what I’m unsure about, and how I might use it later

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 📚 Weekly Learning Entries

::: {.d-grid .gap-2}
[Week 1: Getting Started with Remote Sensing](week1.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [Week 2: Sentinel-2 and Reproducible Research](week2.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [Week 3: Remote Sensing Data and Corrections](week3.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [Week 4: Remote Sensing for Urban Policy](week4.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [Week 5: Google Earth Engine for Remote Sensing Analysis](week5.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [Week 6: Supervised Classification using Google Earth Engine](week6.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [Week 7: Object-based Classification using Google Earth Engine](week7.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [Week 8: Urban Temperature Patterns using Google Earth Engine](week8.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"} [Week 9: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) for Land Surface Analysis](week9.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg role="button"}
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 📝 Module Overview

### Assessment 1 (30%)

-   **Format**: Group presentation (10 minutes)
-   **Topic**: Policy-focused Earth Observation (EO) pitch

### Assessment 2 (70%)

-   **Format**: Weekly learning diary (this site)
